notes: Strongest moment: turn 7 caught the real contradiction — the 'nothing free gets clawed back' claim collides with locking down the undocumented endpoints the same power users scrape — and framed it as the idea's claims colliding, not the person conceding. The concrete falsifier from turn 4 (5% click / <1% conversion by day 60) was correctly treated as a validation-line strength and probed for whether it transfers to the narrower API-only scope, never mislabeled as faith-based; verdict 'sharpened' honestly matches a thesis that genuinely moved (unbundle, API-first, grace period). Weakest moment: turn 9's 'why not ship just the API' skirts close to prescribing a design, but stays a legitimate narrowest-wedge probe since the API is the user's own feature and the user drew the conclusion.
